attack_surface.data_description: High volume of configuration requests to the registry

security_gap.gap_description: The Hyperlane Registry might lack sufficient rate limiting for read requests, making it vulnerable to overload.

actor_risk.description: A malicious Developer or buggy tooling can cause excessive read requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Excessive read requests can overload the registry, denying service to others.

assumptions: The Hyperlane Registry (especially if centralized) is susceptible to overload from a high volume of read requests.
No effective client-side caching or server-side rate-limiting is in place for registry access.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust rate limiting on the Hyperlane Registry. Encourage client-side caching of registry data within SDKs and CLIs. Distribute registry data via CDNs if applicable.

description: A Developer or a misconfigured tool makes an excessive number of read requests to the Hyperlane Registry, potentially overwhelming it and causing a denial of service for other users trying to access configuration data.
